LINEAR_PROGRESSION.description: Increase the load gradually every successful session while maintaining the prescribed repetitions.
LINEAR_PROGRESSION.progressionRule: If all prescribed sets and reps are completed with good technique, increase load by the smallest available increment during the next session.

DOUBLE_PROGRESSION.description: Increase repetitions within a target range before increasing weight.

example.week6: Increase weight and return to 8 reps

RPE_BASED.description: Adjust training load based on Rate of Perceived Exertion.
RPE_BASED.progressionRule: Increase load only when prescribed RPE is achieved consistently.

RIR_BASED.description: Progress while maintaining a target number of repetitions in reserve.
RIR_BASED.progressionRule: Increase load when target repetitions are completed while maintaining the desired RIR.

plateauManagement.definition: A plateau occurs when measurable progress stops despite consistent training and recovery.

COMPOUND.priority: Increase load before adding additional sets.

CORE.priority: Increase exercise difficulty before adding external resistance.

weeklyProgressionRules: Progress only when technique remains consistent.
Never sacrifice range of motion for additional weight.
Small, consistent improvements outperform aggressive load increases.
Missing target repetitions for two consecutive sessions may indicate the need to reduce load.
Recovery quality should influence progression decisions.

llmGuidelines.explanation: Progression recommendations should consider the user's goal, experience level, recovery status and recent performance instead of recommending arbitrary weight increases.
